pattern: Sieve every number up to right for primality, collect the primes inside [left, right], then scan them once keeping the pair with the smallest gap seen so far so ties resolve to the earliest pair.
hints: Generate every prime up to right with a sieve -- trial-dividing each number individually would be far too slow at this range.
Once you have the sorted list of primes inside [left, right], the closest pair is always two consecutive primes in that list -- you never need to compare non-adjacent ones.
Scan the sorted primes once, tracking the smallest gap between consecutive primes seen so far; only replace it on a strictly smaller gap so the first (smallest) pair wins any tie.

edge_inputs.description: left and right are equal and that number is prime, leaving no pair
left and right are equal and that number is not prime
the only even prime creates the smallest possible gap of one
the closest pair sits in the middle of the range, not at either end
the smallest possible range, containing exactly one prime
two adjacent primes at the very bottom of the range
a range with no primes at all

stress.description: random ranges up to the full one-million bound
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    max_val = 1000000
    a = rng.randint(1, max_val)
    b = rng.randint(1, max_val)
    left, right = (a, b) if a <= b else (b, a)
    return [left, right]
